name: New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh
notes:
This rule detects the execution of netsh commands that configure a new port forwarding (PortProxy) rule

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
SIGMA Title: New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXX
SIGMA Description: Detects the execution of netsh commands that configure a new port forwarding (PortProxy) rule


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of netsh

when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%interface%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%portproxy%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%add%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%v4tov4%') OR (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%connectp%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%listena%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%c=%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of netsh

AND when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%interface%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%portproxy%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%add%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%v4tov4%') OR (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%connectp%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%listena%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%c=%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh
Event description: Detects the execution of netsh commands that configure a new port forwarding (PortProxy) rule
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
